title: The Dungeon Dive Collection as of January, 2018
body:
Daniel (@Dungeondive) introduces himself and his Dungeon Dive YouTube channel, sharing his passion for fantasy adventure board games and dungeon crawls. With a curated collection focused on immersive and thematic experiences, Daniel highlights his favorite games, books, and RPG systems, providing insights and recommendations for enthusiasts.

Books

  • Fighting Fantasy Series: Nostalgic gamebooks and their derivatives like Advanced Fighting Fantasy and Lone Wolf.
  • Notable RPG Sourcebooks: Veins of the Earth and Red & Pleasant Land stand out for their imaginative content.
  • Solo-Friendly Systems: Frostgrave and The Quiet Year get a nod for their unique gameplay.

Games

  • Dungeon Crawlers: From Warhammer Quest to Shadows of Brimstone, Daniel shares his favorites for capturing classic dungeon-diving thrills.
  • Underrated Gems: Games like Secrets of the Lost Tomb and DungeonQuest get spotlighted for their niche appeal.
  • Modern Classics: Titles like Gloomhaven, Mansions of Madness 2nd Edition, and This War of Mine showcase a range of storytelling and strategic depth.

Rare Finds

  • City of Chaos: A grail game blending interactive fiction with city exploration.
  • Space Hulk 4th Edition: Praised for its polished components and mechanics.
  • Dungeon Degenerates: Celebrated for its punk-rock aesthetic and heavy ruleset.

Personal Favorites

  • Kingdom Death: Monster: Lauded for its immersive settlement-building and epic encounters.
  • Lords of Vegas: A non-dungeon-crawler pick reflecting Daniel’s diverse gaming taste.

Daniel’s collection offers something for every adventurer—classic, modern, obscure, or thematic. He invites viewers to suggest which games or books to explore further as he shares his passion for storytelling and adventure in the gaming world.
